stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 5 observations with mean 64.3 % (within-factory SD = 9.1). factory B has 15 observations with mean 56.6 % (within-factory SD = 6.6). factory C has 10 observations with mean 44.3 % (within-factory SD = 9.2). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.5 and 16.2 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 63.0 % (shrunk from raw 64.3). Range [61.3, 63.7] as between-factory SD varies from 6.5 to 16.2. Pooled mean: 53.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.8 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=10.8: B = 0.876.
Interval [61.3, 63.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (64.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
